structured_fields.name: top_challenges
structured_fields.description: Major challenges facing the disease area — clinical, scientific, commercial or access related. Each as a concise punchy string.
example: Delayed diagnosis due to heterogeneous symptom presentation
Limited efficacy of current biologics in renal lupus subpopulation
High treatment costs restricting access in low income markets

structured_fields.name: research_gaps
structured_fields.description: Key areas where scientific or clinical evidence is lacking or insufficient. Each as a concise string.
example: Paediatric dosing data for newer biologics
Long term safety data for TYK2 inhibitors in SLE
Biomarkers predicting flare risk in remission patients

structured_fields.name: future_directions
structured_fields.description: Emerging research directions, therapeutic approaches or strategic opportunities likely to shape the field over the next 5-10 years. Each as a concise string.
example: CAR-T cell therapy showing early promise in refractory SLE
Precision medicine approaches using omics profiling for patient stratification
Biosimilar entry expected to reshape market access dynamics post 2026

structured_fields.name: executive_takeaways
structured_fields.description: Short punchy strategic takeaways for a senior pharma or biotech audience — what matters most from this report. Each as a single impactful sentence.
example: The SLE market is poised for significant growth driven by biologic adoption and pipeline maturity
Unmet need remains highest in renal and neuropsychiatric lupus — clear opportunity for differentiation
Early diagnosis investment could unlock substantial patient population currently going untreated

freetext_fields.name: strategic_summary
freetext_fields.description: A rich synthesising narrative that pulls together the most important insights from across the full report — epidemiology, treatment landscape, pipeline and market dynamics. Written for a senior executive audience. Should feel conclusive and forward looking.

prompt_hints.tone: strategic, confident and forward looking — written for a senior pharma or biotech executive audience
avoid: repeating content verbatim from earlier sections
generic insight statements that could apply to any disease
overly cautious language that dilutes strategic impact
introducing new facts not grounded in the context provided
prefer: insights that synthesise across multiple sections rather than summarising one
executive takeaways that are specific, actionable and commercially relevant
future directions grounded in real pipeline and research activity
strategic summary that tells a coherent story not just a list of points
